name: Mauritania
avoid: April onward gets far hotter and windier; by June to September the desert heat can flatten sightseeing and make long 4x4 legs miserable
Do not plan Adrar or Banc d'Arguin without confirmed transport: shared bush taxis are slow, departures are flexible, and a 4x4 is essential once you leave paved roads
Cash is king outside Nouakchott, so sort enough ouguiya before Chinguetti, Iwik or train logistics because ATMs and fuel-stop services are thin
stopoverNote: Nouakchott (NKC) is the practical international gateway. Most independent trips sleep in the capital first, then use a bush taxi or prebooked 4x4 for Banc d'Arguin and the Adrar circuit, because Chinguetti, Ouadane/Richat and Iron Ore Train logistics are far easier once a local driver is locked in.

cities.name: Nouakchott
cities.notes: Windblown capital for the Port de Pêche, National Museum, mosque-lined avenues, seafood restaurants and all the cash-and-transport prep before the desert

cities.name: Chinguetti
cities.notes: UNESCO-listed ksar of sand-filled lanes, family-kept manuscript libraries, old-stone mosques and auberges on the edge of the Sahara dunes

cities.name: Banc d'Arguin National Park
cities.notes: UNESCO-protected coastal park of bird-rich mudflats, Imraguen fishing villages, simple Iwik camp stays and mandatory guided boat access

cities.name: Atar
cities.notes: Adrar hub for Terjit-style oasis detours, desert drivers, Richat Structure planning, Choum train connections and Mauritania's classic auberge stays

itinerary.sim: Mattel and Mauritel are the two easiest prepaid SIM picks; in Nouakchott, budget about ₹440–₹1,100 for a starter pack with enough data for WhatsApp coordination, offline maps, and 4x4 driver calls before coverage thins in Adrar.

days.theme: Land in the capital, sort cash and transport, and ease into Mauritania with the fishing port and a first seafood dinner

activities.name: Taxi from Nouakchott–Oumtounsy International Airport into central Nouakchott
Port de Pêche fish-market and pirogue landing walk
National Museum of Mauritania entry
Grand Mosque / Ibn Abbas Mosque exterior stop and city taxi hops
Cash withdrawal, water and scarf/snack stock-up before the coast or desert

days.theme: Use the capital as a logistics day for the coast or Adrar, with one more museum-and-market pass before the long road

activities.name: Shared bush taxi seat from Nouakchott toward Atar or the northbound transport station
Marché Capitale browse for turbans, dates and desert basics
Optional 4x4 day rental quote check for Adrar or Banc d'Arguin
Tea stop and people-watching on Avenue Gamal Abdel Nasser

days.theme: Leave the capital for the UNESCO coast, then sleep simply in Iwik for the birdlife and tidal-light advantage

activities.name: Shared 4x4 transfer or seat contribution from Nouakchott to Iwik / Banc d'Arguin
Parc National du Banc d'Arguin entry permit
Campement Communautaire d'Iwik bed or simple community-camp stay
Beach and mudflat sunset walk with an Imraguen host or park guide

days.theme: Spend the full day on UNESCO-protected waters with birdlife, dunes and the traditional sailing culture that defines the park

activities.name: Licensed Imraguen boat outing inside Banc d'Arguin
Mandatory guide / permit facilitation share for park access
Birdwatching from the tidal flats and islands
4x4 return toward Nouakchott or onward desert transfer contribution

days.theme: Use Atar as the Adrar staging ground: lock in the 4x4, test your desert rhythm, and scout the town's classic auberges

activities.name: Shared bush taxi seat from Nouakchott to Atar
4x4 rental or driver-and-vehicle day rate for Adrar desert roads
Atar market and old-quarter walk
Short outing to the Azougui ruins or palm groves outside Atar

hotels.name: Hôtel et Auberge Étoile du Nord

days.theme: Pick Mauritania's big desert flex: the Richat loop by 4x4 or the legendary Iron Ore Train setup through Choum and Zouérat

activities.name: Shared 4x4 excursion toward Ouadane and the Richat Structure (Eye of the Sahara)
Guide and lunch share on the Richat / Ouadane circuit
4x4 transfer from Atar to Choum or Zouérat for train logistics
Official passenger-carriage ticket on the Iron Ore Train (Zouérat–Nouadhibou fare; same ballpark if boarding later)

days.theme: Drive into the old ksar, settle into an auberge, and spend the day among manuscript keepers and dune-edged stone lanes

activities.name: Shared 4x4 or bush-taxi transfer from Atar to Chinguetti
Ancient manuscript library visit in Chinguetti
Friday Mosque and old-ksar walking circuit
Guide tip or local host share for dune-edge storytelling and town orientation

days.theme: Keep Chinguetti slow: dawn light on the dunes, a second manuscript stop, and time to appreciate why this remains Mauritania's most atmospheric historic stay

activities.name: Sunrise dune walk above the old town
Second family-library or manuscript-house visit
4x4 half-day outing to the nearby oasis edge or dune camp
Return 4x4 seat toward Atar for onward departures
